title: Expanded filenames should not become options
description: Expanded filenames should not become optionsAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalmaintainabilityCode SmellFilename and pathname should be prefixed to avoid missing filenames starting with a dash when globbing files as program option.Why is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoWithin the command, arguments and filenames as options are passed as strings. Programs may misinterpret files as arguments if the file name starts with a single or double dash. When filenames are specified using glob, files whose names begin with a dash are not included in the scope.
recommendation: Refactor to eliminate the flagged pattern.

checks.message: Expanded filenames should not become options. Glob patterns like '*.txt' should be prefixed with './' (e.g., './*.txt') to prevent files starting with a dash from being misinterpreted as command options. Files like '-file.txt' could be treated as flags instead of filenames.
